other_observers: seen with Nick Komar; found at this location by me
description: the bird was a very large gull, about twice the size of adjacent Ring-billed Gulls. The overall shape was also bulkier than that of Ring-billed Gull, with a heftier body, thicker neck, relatively larger head and thicker bill, and relatively shorter wings. The bill was quite thick relative to it's length (for a gull), yellow in color, with a red spot on the strong gonydeal angle. The eye was relatively small for a gull, and pale colored. I was not close enough to see the orbital ring color.  The head, neck, and underparts were all clean white. The mantle and wings were very dark slate gray, nearly black. The wing tips were black. The bird had a thin white crescent between the mantle and wing tips. The legs were pale pink, and the tail was completely white.Behaviors: The bird was observed loafing the ice with a flock of Ring-billed and Herring Gulls. Towards the end of the observation it flew to the south, but I was not really watching it as I was occupied with a different gull.Call: none heardPlumage: definitive basic
